title: hopea apiculata
intro: Hopea apiculata, also known as Bintangor or Malysian ironwood, is a large evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hopea apiculata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various infections. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of capsules or extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
